article_title: FAA Declares Starship Flight 12 a Mishap and Grounds the Rocket Pending Raptor 3 Engine Investigation
summary: All facts verified across sources; allowlist warnings are policy notes only — no factual issues found

content_preview.title: FAA Declares Starship Flight 12 a Mishap and Grounds the Rocket Pending Raptor 3 Engine Investigation
content_preview.summary: The FAA classified the May 22 Starship V3 test as a mishap on May 27, after Super Heavy Booster 19 failed its boostback burn and hard-splashed in the Gulf of America, grounding the program until SpaceX's investigation is approved.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Federal Aviation Administration declared SpaceX's Starship Flight 12 a mishap on May 27, 2026, five days after the rocket's first version 3 test flight, and ordered the company to complete a supervised investigation before the vehicle can fly again. The agency's formal classification halts Starship operations at a critical moment for SpaceX and for NASA's Artemis lunar program, which depends on the vehicle to land astronauts on the Moon.

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive aerospace news article covering the FAA mishap declaration for Starship Flight 12. Well-structured with event timeline, FAA determination, and forward-looking analysis. 868 words fits the News category.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0.html.gz (Spaceflight Now): confirmed FAA statement ('oversee the SpaceX-led investigation, be involved in every step of the process, and approve SpaceX's final report'), Ship 40 and Booster 20 hardware in flow, 8th mishap investigation context (4 V1 + 3 V2 + this one = 8). source-1.html.gz (SpaceNews): confirmed FAA mishap definition ('failure to complete a launch or reentry as planned'), boostback burn lasted under 20 seconds, 1,500 km/h impact, aircraft departure delays, Blue Origin New Glenn mishap April 19 to May 22 acceptance (33 days calculated from these dates). source-2.html.gz (SpaceQ): confirmed launch time 6:33 PM ET, hydraulic pin scrub, Raptor 3 description, all 33 engines lit, Ship 39 reentry success. source-3.html.gz (TechTimes): confirmed FAA Part 450 regulations, SpaceX no comment, IPO roadshow June 4 / trading June 12 timeline, July-August Flight 13 estimate, Artemis IV implications. source-4.html.gz (Teslarati): confirmed Musk social media quote (two posts combined), 22 objects deployed, 20 Starlink simulators, 2 camera satellites, Indian Ocean splashdown.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key facts verified. The '33 days' Blue Origin comparison is an editorial calculation from SpaceNews dates (April 19 mishap to May 22 investigation accepted) — accurate but not directly stated by SpaceNews. Internal link to prior scrub article exists and is valid. No fabrications detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Complements existing Flight 12 scrub article without duplicating it. New information about the FAA mishap determination.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid aerospace news article with all key facts verified. APPROVE.
